14×3600+44×60+52,1248=53092,1248


A0 is some kind of end of the message bracket
B0101020203030000 is the nrbf message terminator

the 8 bytes before that are a 64 bit little endian unsigned integer (u64::from_le_bytes)

The timestamp will be offset by the id-camera-difference setting (from the displayed timestamp, it sends out a timestamp that has the difference directly applied) - because of this the timestamps above do all not match by exactly 0.1s difference

so, you take the u64::from_le_bytes number, multiply by 100 for a time in ns.
then you get a timestamp by counting these ns from 00:00
